title: relay event: deliver path sends ClearMessage even when MLS active
description: daemon/src/relay.rs dispatch_notification: the DELIVER arm accepts a ClearMessage::Chat payload from the relay and broadcasts it as a MessageReceived event. This path carries unencrypted text in the relay protocol. When MLS is active, the relay should only carry BROADCAST (encrypted) messages; the DELIVER path is the pre-MLS plaintext channel. If both paths are active simultaneously, a client that receives via DELIVER sees unencrypted messages and never knows MLS was bypassed. The daemon does not gate DELIVER handling on mls_active. Trigger: any MLS-capable daemon connected to a relay that still routes plaintext DELIVER. Fix: when mls_client is set and an MLS group is established, reject or warn on DELIVER payloads that are valid ClearMessage::Chat (they should have arrived as BROADCAST/MLS).
